title: "No preview available yet" empty state handling
description: Polish and unify the empty-state UX across editor and public views for a component-embed whose referenced component/variant has no published preview (never published, or the reference is stale/renamed), ensuring it never renders a broken iframe.
acceptanceCriteria: In the editor, a component-embed with no resolvable preview shows a distinct, clearly-labeled state (e.g. 'Nenhum preview publicado para "Button / destructive" ainda') different from the initial 'not yet selected' placeholder from TASK-29
The same empty state (adapted for read-only context, no 'select component' action) appears on the public page (TASK-50) if a published page references a component/variant with no available preview
The empty state never attempts to render an `<iframe>` with an invalid/empty `src`
A 'refresh'/'tentar novamente' action is available in the editor's empty state, re-querying `componentPreviews.getLatest` in case the CI upload landed after the page was opened

steps.description: Distinguish the two empty states in the NodeView
steps.details: In `ComponentEmbed`'s NodeView (TASK-47), differentiate between `componentName === ''` (never selected — TASK-29's original placeholder, prompts to open the picker) and `componentName set but getLatest returned null` (referenced but unpublished — this task's new state).

steps.description: Design and implement the unpublished-reference empty state
steps.details: Render a distinct box (e.g. different icon/color from the 'not selected' placeholder) with the component/variant name and a message indicating no preview has been published yet.
Add a 'Tentar novamente' button that re-triggers the `componentPreviews.getLatest` query for that node, useful right after a team's CI finishes publishing.

steps.description: Reuse the same empty state on the public page
steps.details: In `PageRenderer.tsx` (TASK-50), reuse the same unpublished-reference empty-state component in read-only mode (no picker/retry action needed there, just the informative message).

Using Playwright: insert a component-embed referencing a component/variant with no published preview, confirm the distinct empty state renders (not the 'not selected' one and not a broken iframe); publish a matching artifact, click 'Tentar novamente', and confirm it now resolves to the real preview.

technicalNotes: This task is primarily UX polish over behavior already partially covered in TASK-47/TASK-29 — its job is making the three distinct states (not selected / selected-but-unpublished / resolved) visually and behaviorally unambiguous, not introducing new data flows.
